question: When will the front-month WTI crude oil price return to our defined normalization threshold (WTI ≤ $68.26/bbl)?
key_assumptions: Event fires when WTI falls below τ
Historical price dynamics (2007-2026) are representative of future behavior
No structural breaks or regime changes in mean-reversion parameters
Log-normal price distribution (removed 2 negative price observations)
key_uncertainties: Correct threshold τ — sensitivity to this choice
Mean-reversion assumption may not hold if structural regime shift occurred in 2026
Estimated half-life of ~198 days implies slow reversion
2026 partial-year shows elevated volatility and higher mean vs 2025
Geopolitical shocks or supply disruptions could break historical patterns
